islands.name: Business Bay
islands.description: Run a small business: manage inventory, track cash flow, and understand depreciation.

learning_objectives: Understand business inventory and cash flow management
Learn about asset depreciation and capital expenses
Practice profit-and-loss tracking for a small business
provenance.originality_notes: Theme: island bay with workshops and warehouses (not a corporate office sim). Verb: manage inventory + negotiate with NPCs (not spreadsheet simulation). Structure: story-driven quests teaching one concept per quest (not sandbox management). Combines resource management with trade negotiation through NPC-guided scenarios.

areas.name: Storefront
areas.description: Your shop's front counter. Manage inventory, set prices, and serve customers.

areas.name: Back Office
areas.description: Desks covered in spreadsheets. Track cash flow, profit margins, and expenses.

areas.name: Warehouse
areas.description: Shelves of equipment and stock. Learn about assets, depreciation, and write-offs.

items.name: Business Ledger
items.description: A record of all business income and expenses.

items.name: Store Equipment
items.description: A depreciating asset — worth less every year on the books.

items.name: Cash Flow Report
items.description: Shows money in vs money out. Positive cash flow keeps the business alive.

items.name: Business Bay Medallion
items.description: Proof of business acumen. Unlocks the next island!

quests.title: Inventory Hustle
quests.description: Stock your store, price items for profit, and manage inventory turnover without running out or overstocking.
quests.hint: Talk to Shop Owner Sam to learn pricing, then negotiate with Supplier Steve for bulk deals.

quests.title: Depreciation & Write-offs
quests.description: Understand how business assets lose value over time and how that affects taxes and cash flow.
quests.hint: Visit Accountant Amy to learn about cash flow, then see Tax Advisor Tina for depreciation rules.

nodes.speaker: Shop Owner Sam
nodes.text: Running a shop is all about margins. Buy low, sell high, and make sure you don't run out of stock OR drown in unsold inventory. Want to learn the ropes?

choices.text: Teach me about pricing and inventory!

nodes.speaker: Shop Owner Sam
nodes.text: Rule of thumb: your markup should cover not just the product cost, but also rent, wages, and a profit margin. Aim for at least 30% gross margin. Now go talk to Supplier Steve about getting inventory.

nodes.speaker: Shop Owner Sam
nodes.text: Come back anytime — the store's always open!

nodes.speaker: Accountant Amy
nodes.text: Cash flow is the lifeblood of any business. Revenue means nothing if you can't pay your bills THIS month. Want to see how it works?

choices.text: What's the difference between profit and cash flow?

nodes.speaker: Accountant Amy
nodes.text: I'll prepare a cash flow report for you. But also talk to Tina about depreciation — it's a non-cash expense that reduces your tax bill. Sounds boring, but it saves real money.

nodes.speaker: Accountant Amy
nodes.text: Profit is revenue minus expenses on paper. But cash flow is actual money in and out. A business can be 'profitable' on paper but run out of cash if customers pay late. Cash is king!

nodes.speaker: Supplier Steve
nodes.text: Need stock? I've got three pricing tiers: small batch (high price per unit), bulk (cheaper per unit but more cash upfront), and on-demand (fastest but most expensive). What'll it be?

nodes.speaker: Supplier Steve
nodes.text: Bulk saves you 25% per unit, but you need to sell it all before it expires or becomes obsolete. Dead inventory is dead money.

nodes.speaker: Supplier Steve
nodes.text: Start small to test demand, then scale up. The worst mistake is ordering 1,000 units of something nobody wants. Ready to try the cashflow simulator?

nodes.speaker: Tax Advisor Tina
nodes.text: Depreciation is how businesses account for assets losing value over time. A $10,000 machine that lasts 5 years? You can deduct $2,000/year from your taxes. It's like a discount from the government for investing in your business.

choices.text: So it saves me money on taxes?

nodes.speaker: Tax Advisor Tina
nodes.text: Exactly! Here's your cash flow report showing how depreciation affects your bottom line. The equipment isn't worth less in reality — it's an accounting tool that reduces taxable income.

nodes.speaker: Tax Advisor Tina
nodes.text: A write-off means you deduct a business expense from your taxable income. But remember — a write-off isn't 'free money.' You still spent the money, you just pay less tax on it. A $1,000 write-off in a 25% tax bracket saves you $250.

choices.text: Got it! Show me the report.

minigames.name: Cash Flow Micro-Sim
minigames.description: Run a small store for 12 simulated months. Balance inventory costs, revenue, and expenses to stay cash-positive.

minigames.name: Set the Price
minigames.description: Price the products in your store. Slide the price and watch foot traffic and profit move in real time to find the sweet spot.
